questions.question: Which empire built the Taj Mahal in India?
questions.explanation: The Taj Mahal was commissioned by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in 1632 as a mausoleum for his beloved wife Mumtaz Mahal, who died during childbirth. It took approximately 22 years and 20,000 workers to complete. The white marble structure in Agra, India, is considered the greatest example of Mughal architecture and one of the New Seven Wonders of the World.
questions.memory_hook: Taj Mahal = Mughal Emperor's tribute to his wife. She died in childbirth with their 14th child. He built the most beautiful building in the world for her. 22 years, 20,000 workers. Shah Jahan was later imprisoned by his own son and could only see the Taj from his prison window.

questions.question: The first telephone call in history was made by Alexander Graham Bell in 1876. What were his first words?
options: Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you.
questions.answer: Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you.
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell made the first successful telephone call on March 10, 1876, saying 'Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you.' to his assistant Thomas Watson, who was in the next room. Bell had received his patent for the telephone just three days earlier. The telephone transformed human communication more than any invention since writing.
questions.memory_hook: First phone call: 'Mr. Watson, come here. I want to see you.' March 10, 1876. Watson was in the next room. The first thing ever said on a phone was essentially 'come here.' Bell accidentally spilled acid on himself during the call.

questions.question: What Afrikaans-derived word names the system of institutionalized racial segregation enforced by the South African government from 1948 until its repeal in the early 1990s?
questions.explanation: Apartheid — Afrikaans for 'separateness' — was a system of racial segregation in South Africa enforced through legislation from 1948 to 1991. It classified citizens by race and restricted where they could live, work, and travel. Nelson Mandela spent 27 years in prison for opposing it.

questions.question: What is the term in baseball for a batter being hit by a pitched ball?
questions.explanation: 'Hit by pitch' (HBP) occurs when a pitched ball strikes the batter without the batter swinging, awarding the batter first base. The batter must make an attempt to avoid being hit — if they deliberately lean into the pitch, it can be ruled a non-event. It is abbreviated HBP or HP in scoring. Being hit by a fastball traveling 95+ mph is painful regardless of padded batting gloves.
questions.memory_hook: Hit by pitch = free base in baseball. The batter must try to avoid it — leaning into a 95 mph pitch on purpose is rare, painful, and technically against the rules. Some batters are notorious for getting hit often; others are almost never hit.

questions.question: How long is a marathon running race?
questions.explanation: A marathon is 26 miles and 385 yards (42.195 km). The distance was standardized at the 1908 London Olympics, set to run from Windsor Castle to the Olympic stadium and finish in front of the royal box. The race commemorates the legend of Pheidippides, a Greek messenger who allegedly ran from Marathon to Athens (about 25 miles) to announce a military victory in 490 BC, then collapsed and died.
questions.memory_hook: Marathon = 26.2 miles. Standardized because of the British royal family's preference for the 1908 finish line. Named after a battle in ancient Greece. The original messenger legend may be apocryphal — but the race is very real.

questions.question: Which legendary filmmaker directed 2001: A Space Odyssey (1968) and The Shining (1980)?
questions.explanation: Stanley Kubrick is one of cinema's most celebrated auteurs, directing films across multiple genres including Dr. Strangelove, 2001: A Space Odyssey, A Clockwork Orange, Barry Lyndon, The Shining, Full Metal Jacket, and Eyes Wide Shut. He was known for extreme perfectionism — some takes ran into the hundreds. He died in 1999, just days after showing his final cut of Eyes Wide Shut to Warner Bros.
questions.memory_hook: Kubrick = 2001 + The Shining + Full Metal Jacket. A perfectionist who once did 127 takes of an actress walking through a door. He made films in totally different genres — all look unmistakably like Kubrick. Died 4 days after screening his final film.

questions.question: 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' by Nirvana became the anthem of which musical movement?
questions.explanation: 'Smells Like Teen Spirit,' released in September 1991, became the defining song of grunge — a subgenre of alternative rock from Seattle characterized by distorted guitars, angst-filled lyrics, and rejection of mainstream pop aesthetics. The song's music video received heavy rotation on MTV and helped Nirvana's album Nevermind displace Michael Jackson's Dangerous from the top of the charts.
questions.memory_hook: 'Smells Like Teen Spirit' = grunge anthem. Seattle sound. Knocked Michael Jackson off #1. Kurt Cobain reportedly hated how famous it became — he felt it misrepresented what the band was about. The irony of an anti-commercial song becoming the most commercial song of its era.

questions.question: True Detective Season 1 (HBO, 2014), starring Matthew McConaughey and Woody Harrelson, is considered a landmark of prestige TV. What distinguished it narratively and thematically?
options: It was the first TV drama to be written entirely by one author in a single creative session — Nic Pizzolatto wrote all 8 episodes in 6 weeks, giving it an unusually unified philosophical voice

Its nonlinear structure alternated between a 1995 murder investigation and a 2012 interrogation of the same detectives — gradually revealing that the investigators themselves are as unreliable as their suspects, against a backdrop of Louisiana gothic horror

It featured the first major TV performance delivered entirely through improvisation — McConaughey's Rust Cohle had no scripted lines, working only from character notes and improvising in response to Harrelson
It pioneered the anthology format for premium TV — the first series to use entirely new characters, setting, and story each season while keeping the same title
questions.answer: Its nonlinear structure alternated between a 1995 murder investigation and a 2012 interrogation of the same detectives — gradually revealing that the investigators themselves are as unreliable as their suspects, against a backdrop of Louisiana gothic horror
questions.explanation: True Detective Season 1 used a double-timeline structure that made the viewer simultaneously live through the investigation in 1995 and interrogate the investigators' memories in 2012 — raising the question of what really happened and what was invented in retrospect. McConaughey's Rust Cohle delivered bleak philosophical monologues that became cultural touchstones ('Time is a flat circle'). Director Cary Fukunaga shot the entire season, giving it visual coherence rare in TV. The 6-minute tracking shot through a housing project in Episode 4 remains one of TV's most technically acclaimed sequences.
questions.memory_hook: True Detective's genius was its double timeline — watching 2012 detectives interrogate their 1995 selves made both the suspects and the investigators unreliable. Episode 4's unbroken 6-minute tracking shot through a drug den is still one of TV's most cited set pieces.

questions.question: Lake Titicaca, the highest navigable lake in the world, sits on the border of which two countries?
questions.explanation: Lake Titicaca sits at 3,812 meters above sea level on the Andes plateau, straddling the border of Peru and Bolivia. It is the largest lake in South America by volume and the highest navigable lake in the world. The Uros people have lived on floating reed islands on the lake for centuries. Its name is believed to mean 'rock of the puma' in Quechua.
questions.memory_hook: Lake Titicaca = Peru and Bolivia, 3,812 meters up. The highest navigable lake on Earth. Floating reed islands with people living on them. The lake is so large it moderates the climate of the surrounding plateau.

questions.question: What is the name for the protective ozone layer primarily made of?
questions.explanation: The ozone layer is made up of ozone molecules, each consisting of three oxygen atoms (O3). Normal atmospheric oxygen has two atoms (O2). Ozone is created when UV radiation splits O2 molecules, and the free oxygen atoms bond with other O2 molecules. The ozone layer absorbs most of the Sun's UV-B and UV-C radiation, protecting life on Earth from DNA damage and skin cancer.
questions.memory_hook: Ozone = O3. Three oxygen atoms. Normal air is O2 (two oxygen atoms). The ozone layer is O3 molecules absorbing UV radiation that would otherwise fry life on Earth. CFCs break the O3 back into O2, depleting the protective layer.

questions.question: The Carpathian Mountains form a curved arc through eight Central and Eastern European countries. Which of these countries is NOT part of the Carpathians?
questions.explanation: The Carpathian Mountains form a 1,500 km arc through Czech Republic, Slovakia, Poland, Ukraine, Romania, Hungary, Serbia, and Austria. Romania contains the largest portion (the Transylvanian Alps are part of the Carpathians). The range is home to Europe's largest population of brown bears, wolves, lynx, and European bison. Bram Stoker set Dracula's castle in the Carpathians, in Transylvania, Romania.
questions.memory_hook: Carpathians: curved 1,500 km mountain arc through 8 Central/Eastern European countries. NOT Spain. Romania has the most. Europe's largest populations of bears, wolves, and lynx live here. Transylvania (Dracula country) is in the Romanian Carpathians. The most biodiverse mountain range in Europe.

questions.question: What is the term for atoms of the same element with different numbers of neutrons — some of which are stable and some radioactive?
questions.explanation: Isotopes are atoms of the same element (same number of protons) with different numbers of neutrons. Carbon-12 (stable) and carbon-14 (radioactive, used in dating) are both carbon isotopes. Hydrogen has three isotopes: protium (no neutrons), deuterium (1 neutron), and tritium (2 neutrons, radioactive). Medical imaging uses radioactive isotopes: iodine-131 for thyroid treatment; technetium-99m for PET scans. The mass number of an isotope = protons + neutrons.
questions.memory_hook: Isotopes: same element, different neutron count. Carbon-12 (stable) vs. Carbon-14 (radioactive → used in dating). Hydrogen has 3 isotopes including deuterium (in 'heavy water'). Medicine uses radioactive isotopes: iodine-131 for thyroid, technetium-99m for scans. Same atom, different behavior.
